source_basis: German public PV and heat-pump reviews
consumer guidance on PV offers, rental models, heat pumps, and sales practices

objections.name: Upfront price shock
customer_wording: geht da noch was am Preis
objections.underlying_concern: The customer may not understand the scope, may be comparing non-equivalent offers, or may have a real budget ceiling.
proof_that_helps: Comparison of included and excluded services
Alternative package or phased option if scope is flexible

best_task_types.Phone Call: High for live clarification when price shock is emotionally charged or competitor comparison exists.
best_task_types.Send Email: High for written scope breakdown and revised option recap.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Medium only if price depends on site-specific uncertainties.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium for a short personalized explanation of what changed or what is included.
best_task_types.Send Gift: Very low; does not solve price shock.

what_not_to_say: Do not discount before explaining scope.
Do not imply the quote is cheap without context.
Do not dismiss a competitor offer.

objections.name: Liquidity or financing concern
objections.underlying_concern: The customer may like the system but cannot or does not want to pay the full amount upfront.
proof_that_helps: Buy versus finance versus phased-scope view

best_task_types.Phone Call: Medium when the customer needs a confidential budget discussion.
best_task_types.Send Email: High for a clear finance explainer and total-cost comparison.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Low unless financing is tied to broader household planning.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Low to medium for logistics, not detailed finance terms.

what_not_to_say: Do not imply finance approval.
Do not use stale rates.
Do not make monthly cost look lower by hiding total cost or obligations.
missing_data_to_check: Customer preference for purchase versus finance

objections.name: ROI or payback distrust
objections.underlying_concern: The customer does not trust the calculation or does not understand which assumptions drive it.
proof_that_helps: Comparison against no-battery or battery-later option

best_task_types.Phone Call: High for a 10-20 minute assumptions review.
best_task_types.Send Email: High for the assumptions table and recap.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Medium if assumptions depend on roof or consumption data not yet validated.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium for a personal screen-recorded walkthrough if consent exists.

what_not_to_say: Do not guarantee payback.
Do not imply electricity prices will rise by a fixed amount without source.
Do not hide uncertainty.

objections.name: Competing offer comparison
objections.underlying_concern: The customer needs to compare scope, quality, risk, assumptions, and price without being forced into a yes/no decision.

best_task_types.Meeting in person: Medium when comparison depends on site condition.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium for quick personalized comparison summary.
best_task_types.Send Gift: Low; can feel like a distraction.

what_not_to_say: Do not badmouth competitors.
Do not assume competitor offer is incomplete without evidence.
Do not pressure the customer to stop comparing.

objections.name: Proposal clarity or bundle suspicion
objections.underlying_concern: The customer suspects that bundled pricing hides assumptions, unnecessary add-ons, or missing services.
proof_that_helps: Component, labor, scaffolding, grid/admin, and optional add-on breakdown

best_task_types.Send Email: Very high for itemized written addendum.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Low unless physical inspection is part of the missing clarity.

what_not_to_say: Do not say all included without naming what is included.
Do not avoid line-item questions.
Do not present too many variants without a recommended path.

objections.name: Installer trust or pressure concern
objections.underlying_concern: The buyer is judging whether the installer is respectful, accountable, and reliable.
proof_that_helps: Specific answer to the customer's last question

best_task_types.Phone Call: High if consent exists and the buyer needs a human trust repair.
best_task_types.Send Email: High for written agenda and responsibility proof.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: High if trust concern is tied to house-specific risk.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium if personal human presence helps.

what_not_to_say: Do not manufacture urgency.
Do not ignore the tone issue.
Do not switch contacts without explanation.

objections.name: Responsiveness or continuity gap
objections.underlying_concern: The customer fears that questions and post-signature issues will not be owned.

best_task_types.Phone Call: High for urgent unresolved questions if consent exists.
best_task_types.Send Email: High for written answer and owner confirmation.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Medium if gap concerns field visit or technical proof.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium for quick personal answer if opted in.

what_not_to_say: Do not send a marketing message before answering the open question.
Do not promise response times the installer cannot meet.
Do not make ownership ambiguous.

objections.name: Battery value or sizing doubt
objections.underlying_concern: The customer likes independence but doubts whether the battery size, timing, or economics fit their actual usage.
proof_that_helps: Future EV or heat-pump load scenario

best_task_types.Phone Call: Medium to high for scenario discussion.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Medium if site or load data is missing.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: High for visual scenario walkthrough if opted in.

what_not_to_say: Do not claim 100% independence.
Do not claim battery payback without assumptions.
Do not treat autonomy and ROI as the same motive.

objections.name: Winter, cloud, or shade performance concern
objections.underlying_concern: The customer worries that the system will underperform during the months or conditions that matter most.

best_task_types.Send Email: High for seasonal chart and assumptions.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: High if shade or roof conditions need inspection.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: High for visual explanation of monthly output.

what_not_to_say: Do not say panels simply work in winter without explaining lower output.
Do not imply full heat-pump coverage in winter.
Do not ignore site-specific shade.

objections.name: Heat-pump monthly burden or building fit
objections.underlying_concern: The buyer is uncertain whether the heat pump fits the building and what it will do to monthly energy costs.

best_task_types.Phone Call: High after key data is available.
best_task_types.Send Email: High for missing-data request and scenario recap.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: High if building fit cannot be assessed remotely.

what_not_to_say: Do not send generic heat-pump savings claims.
Do not ignore missing heat-load data.
Do not claim a subsidy or tariff without checking current conditions.

objections.name: Roof or property damage risk
objections.underlying_concern: The customer fears that installation will damage the home or create warranty/insurance disputes.

best_task_types.Phone Call: Medium if consent exists and concern needs explanation.
best_task_types.Send Email: High for checklist and written boundaries.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Very high if physical validation is needed.

what_not_to_say: Do not say there is no risk.
Do not give vague reassurance.
Do not skip inspection when the physical concern is central.

objections.name: Paperwork, grid, or admin uncertainty
objections.underlying_concern: The customer wants to know who handles administrative steps and which delays are external.

best_task_types.Meeting in person: Low unless combined with site visit.

what_not_to_say: Do not promise exact external approval dates.
Do not hide customer-required steps.
Do not use vague all-in promises.

objections.name: Aftercare or support concern
objections.underlying_concern: The buyer wants to know what support, handover, monitoring, and warranty look like after installation.
proof_that_helps: Named support contact or escalation route

best_task_types.Send Email: High for written support and handover explanation.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Medium if combined with site visit or final review.

what_not_to_say: Do not use vague all-in reassurance.
Do not promise support speed that is not real.
Do not confuse installation proof with aftercare proof.

objections.name: Stakeholder or advisor review
objections.underlying_concern: The customer needs another decision-maker or advisor to understand and approve the offer.
proof_that_helps: ROI and impact summary if motives differ

best_task_types.Phone Call: High for joint review if consent and availability exist.
best_task_types.Meeting in person: Medium if household or physical inspection matters.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: High for partner-shareable recap if opted in.

what_not_to_say: Do not infer family status without explicit mention.
Do not pressure one person to decide alone.
Do not overload the second stakeholder with raw proposal detail.

objections.name: Needs site visit
objections.underlying_concern: The customer does not trust remote assumptions enough and wants the house-specific facts validated.

best_task_types.Phone Call: Medium as scheduling and agenda setup.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Low to medium for pre-visit context.

what_not_to_say: Do not keep sending remote reassurance if the customer requested inspection.
Do not schedule without a purpose.
Do not use a visit for low-readiness leads with no physical blocker.

objections.name: No response or ghosting risk
objections.underlying_concern: The blocker is unknown; the customer may be comparing, waiting for another stakeholder, overwhelmed, or no longer interested.

best_task_types.Phone Call: Low unless requested or clearly consented and high value.
best_task_types.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Low unless opted in and specific value exists.

what_not_to_say: Do not use artificial deadlines.
Do not send repeated generic nudges.
Do not use opens or views without tracking consent.
